power: USB-C 5V → AP2112-3.3 LDO → 3.3V rail (MCU, flash, peripherals)
features: WS2812B RGB status LED on GPIO pin
rationale: The RP2040 provides excellent price-to-performance ratio with dual cores, flexible PIO state machines, and a mature SDK ecosystem. The AP2112 LDO was chosen for its low dropout voltage and 600mA output, sufficient for the RP2040 plus flash and LED. USB-C replaces the legacy micro-B connector for modern convenience. The W25Q128 provides 16MB of flash, the standard capacity for Pico-compatible boards.
risks: RP2040 stock availability may be constrained during high-demand periods
USB-C connector requires precise soldering — consider designing for selective soldering
QSPI signal integrity at 133 MHz requires controlled trace impedance on 2-layer board
